content: Just as the linguistic meaning of "reflexion" is learned and reinforced through repeated use in high school English writing and reading workshops to build deliberate reflective thinking habits, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be redesigned to incorporate spaced, scaffolded formative practice of self-critique across different task types, rather than only adding single-step reflection after generation. This structured skill-building approach mirrors how human learners internalize reflective practice, rather than treating reflection as an innate capability of large models.
